turn-1-full.text: To do or to make in Spanish is hacer. It has a silent h, spelled h-a-c-e-r. In most of the Spanish-speaking world, it's pronounced ha/s/er. What is the verb ending?

turn-3-reveal.text: verb. Whenever we come across a new verb, note the ending. If we know it's an
verb, we know how to work with it.

turn-3-prompt.text: How would you say 'he's doing' or 'she's making'?

turn-5-prompt.text: How would you say 'you're doing it'?

turn-7-prompt.text: How would you say 'they're doing it' or 'you guys are doing it'?

turn-9-prompt.text: How do you say 'you're doing it' informally?

turn-11-reveal.text: Good. The word for 'what' in Spanish is 'qué'.

turn-11-prompt.text: How do you say 'what are you doing'?

turn-13-full.text: ¿Qué haces? This can also mean 'how are you' or 'what are you up to'. For -ar verbs, we add 'n' for 'they' or 'you (plural)', and 's' for informal 'you'. For -er/-ir verbs, we add 's' for informal 'you', 'n' for 'they', 'you (plural)'.

turn-14-full.text: Puedo in Spanish means 'I can' or 'I am able'. How would you say 'he can' or 'she can'?

turn-18-prompt.text: How would you say 'can you come?' informally?

turn-24-prompt.text: I want to know why you can't come.

turn-26-reveal.text: Quiero saber por qué no puedes venir.
Well done. Let's recap: for -ar verbs, we remove the ending for 'I' and add 'o'. For -er/-ir verbs, we do the same.

turn-26-prompt.text: Vender is 'to sell', and 'I sell' is:

turn-28-prompt.text: Comprar is 'to buy', and 'I buy' is:

turn-30-prompt.text: Llegar is 'to arrive', and 'I arrive' is:

turn-32-full.text: Some verbs are irregular, like salir, which means 'to leave'. If we apply the rule, we might say 'salo', but it's actually 'salgo'.

turn-33-full.text: Esperar means 'to wait' or 'to hope'. How do you say 'I wait' or 'I hope'?

turn-39-prompt.text: If you want to say 'they're waiting for me', you say '

turn-41-prompt.text: How do you say 'they're not waiting for me'?

turn-43-prompt.text: I want to know why they're not waiting for me.

turn-45-full.text: Quiero saber por qué no me esperan.
Well done. Words ending in -ary become -ario in Spanish. For example, 'necessary' becomes 'necesario'.

outro-1-full.text: You've learned how to use the verb 'hacer' and understand verb conjugations for different subjects.

outro-2-full.text: Try using 'hacer' in different sentences. How would you say 'we are making it' or 'they are doing it'?

outro-3-full.text: Practice these conjugations with other verbs you know. The more you use them, the more natural they'll become.
